Bus Timings

Abu Dhabi N43 Bus Timings from Corniche Hospital to Qaryat Al Beri Souq

June 29, 2025
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

Bus N43 (overnight) is one of the main routes, running from Corniche Hospital to Qaryat Al Beri Souq. This is an overnight service, ensuring you can travel easily across the city at any hour. The following guide is a detailed walkthrough of the N43 bus route, with timings, major stops, and some useful travel tips. This information allows passengers to enjoy smooth and efficient travel.

Contents

Operational Details

The bus N43 runs 24 hours a day and is therefor the perfect connection for locals as well as tourists. Below is the detailed schedule for Bus N43, departing from both Corniche Hospital and Qaryat Al Beri Souq:

From Corniche Hospital From Qaryat Al Beri Souq
00:03 00:07
01:03 01:08
02:02 02:11
03:02 03:11
04:01 04:09

Bus N43 TimetableFor the full schedule, visit the official AD Mobility website or follow this link for the N43 Bus Timings.

Ticket Pricing: Bus N43 Travel payments can be made using Abu Dhabi’s convenient cashless payment system, the Hafilat Card. For single zone journeys, fares are AED 2 and an additional AED 0.05 per kilometer for additional distance. For a cashless, hassle free experience, passengers can top up their Hafilat cards online or at kiosks across the city.

Route Overview

The N43 bus route serves some of the most important stops, connecting some of the most important sites, cultural sites, and amenities. Here’s a breakdown of the key stops along the N43 route:

  1. Corniche Hospital (Start): The journey begins at a major healthcare facility.

  2. Al Mina Street: A bustling area with shops and local attractions.

  3. Hamdan Street: A prominent commercial street offering various services.

  4. Hazaa Bin Zayed Mosque: A notable religious landmark in the city.

  5. Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Street: A major thoroughfare with easy access to public services.

  6. Al Mariah Mall: A popular shopping and dining destination.

  7. Fatimah Bint Mubarak Street: A busy road connecting residential areas.

  8. Sultan Bin Zayed The First Street: Offering access to local shops and businesses.

  9. Liwa Center: A shopping complex known for local goods.

  10. Sheikh Zayed 1st Mosque: Another important religious site along the route.

  11. Etisalat: A stop for telecom services.

  12. Al Falah Street: A key area for restaurants and shops.

  13. Al Tabbiyah Mosque: A cultural and spiritual stop.

  14. SKMC: A major hospital serving the local community.

  15. Hazaa Bin Zayed Street: A busy road with shops and services.

  16. Omar Bin Al Khatab Mosque: A well-known mosque along the route.

  17. Abu Dhabi Food Control Authority: A stop serving government and regulatory services.

  18. Delma Street: A commercial area with various shops and restaurants.

  19. ITC Bus Depot: A major transportation hub.

  20. Mohammed Bin Khalifa Street: A central street with government offices.

  21. Ministry of Information & Culture: A significant government institution.

  22. Al Jazeera Sports Club: A popular spot for sports and recreation.

  23. Immigration Department: A key service stop for visa-related services.

  24. Khalifa University: A notable stop for students and academics.

  25. Wedding Hall: A venue for events and celebrations.

Recommended Guides

Enhance your daily commute with these essential transport guides:

Complete RTA Intercity Routes Guide Largest Bus Stations Infrastructure in UAE

  1. Al Nahda National School: An important educational stop.

  2. Salama Bint Butti Street: A key road for local access.

  3. Al Ghaf Park: A recreational area for families.

  4. Mishrif Mall: A popular shopping destination.

  5. Al Dhafra Street: Connecting residential and business areas.

  6. Mawgab Street: A local community access point.

  7. Rabdan Street: Another key area for residential and commercial access.

  8. Embassies Area: A district housing various international embassies.

  9. Sultan Bin Zayed Street: A major road serving both residential and business needs.

  10. General Civil Aviation Authority: A stop for aviation-related services.

  11. Saif Ghobash Street: A central road for business and local traffic.

  12. Mzaham Street: An important residential and business area.

  13. Zayed Sports Stadium: A major sports venue.

  14. Al Noor Hospital: A key healthcare facility.

  15. Ministry of Interior: Another government institution on the route.

41.

  1. Al Maqta Bus Interchange B: A major bus transfer point.

  2. Healthcare Center: A stop for medical services.

  3. Coop: A local retail destination.

  4. Al Itihad Street: A major road connecting key areas.

  5. Street 17: A neighborhood access point.

  6. Al Khor Street: Connecting local communities.

  7. ITC: Another major transportation center.

  8. Qaryat Al Beri Souq (End): The final destination for shopping and leisure.

Among other bus routes that you can check in Abu Dhabi are the N41 overnight bus that run along Corniche Hospital to ADNEC.

Nearby Attractions and Landmarks

Passengers traveling on Bus N43 can easily access several popular attractions in Abu Dhabi:

1.

  1. Zayed Sports Stadium: A hub for major sporting events and concerts.

  2. Qaryat Al Beri Souq: A vibrant marketplace offering shopping, dining, and cultural experiences.

Journey Experience

But a long and comfortable bus ride from Corniche Hospital on Bus N43 to Qaryat Al Beri Souq, which runs through the heart of Abu Dhabi, provided a glimpse at the heart of this growing city. The bus going along major zones like Hamdan Street, nearly ticking off some popular locations like the Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque, is literally a must for tourists, meant to admire the jaw dropping architecture.

For shopping fans, Al Mariah Mall and Mishrif Mall also passed on the route.

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)

Editorial review date: June 29, 2025.

Written by Al Khaleej
